From what you have told me, this Doctor wants to stop Arctosz from being the reckless man he is and launching an all-out war. Considering that, it's not likely there will be any direct confrontation that leads to bloodshed.

I don't exactly believe in this possibility, however. The way I see it, it's not out of the question that this Doctor is trying to take control of the Paleroches and the Browntails, seeking to become Kjerag's new overlord.
The Doctor's not that kind of person.
Right. The Doctor certainly could do it, though.
I must say I'm a little flabbergasted by the appraisal that you two and Enciodes have of this person.
I can only hope you are right in that judgment.
In any case, however things end up, it's very likely that they are aiming for Mount Karlan, which is under our control, of course.
They'll need the Saintess to turn the tide of public opinion.
And if they succeed in getting the Saintess out of Mount Karlan, then all that Enciodes has done will be for naught.
Naturally, the Doctor knows that.
That's why we will see this Doctor here.
It's ironic. In their eyes, we Silverashes are taking our own family heiress hostage.
They aren't wrong. Enya Silverash is indeed our hostage right now.
If you would rather I use a different turn of phrase, the Saintess has become our most important bargaining chip.
That doesn't change anything, however.
Gnosis... could you please stop provoking Matterhorn?
I'm helping you two face reality.
Or are you trying to tell me that you two didn't know that Enciodes had his eyes on more than just the transfer of powers?
It was never his plan to let faith govern the state.
That's not to say anything negative about Enya Silverash.
I'm not interested in her at all. Naturally, I don't have any opinion about her.
But she became the Saintess, and she's done very well.

In that case, she will no doubt be an obstacle to Enciodes. That's all. And now things have come to this, we cannot permit anyone else on this stage to do any better than the head of the Silverash family.
I know that...
Of course I know that.
This isn't a game. There are no do-overs. Let's not give ourselves any unnecessary trouble.
Monch, you're in charge of surveillance.
On it.
Good. This plan is perfect!
You sure about that...?

opts: Of course.

lines.x: On the surface, we're sending our forces to raze the place to the ground, making it look like we're there to save Yucatan and attract their attention that way, to confuse them about your real plans.
You want the main force to attack them from the front.
That's to let Arctosz's forces get near Mount Karlan. Your guys will also be there in the mountains to help them along.
Doesn't sound like the safest plan there is, but it seems feasible.
What about Degenbrecher? Even if you can deal with Enciodes's Tschäggättä performers, if you don't have a way to take care of that actual monster, you'll never be able to get to him.

opts: Sharp.

lines.x: Is this part of my job too?

opts: Yes.

lines.x: You make that sound simple, but are you sure about this?
I wouldn't say sure. But I can probably keep her busy for some ten minutes.
You're a monster yourself, huh...?
So, Doctor of Rhodes Island. How are you so sure I'll help you?

opts: I'm not forcing you.
There isn't any part of this plan that has to involve you.

lines.x: You know, you kind of remind me of Enciodes.
You told me all about your plan, and then you say don't absolutely need me.
But I already know every last detail.
No one would let somebody who knows so much just walk away.
Whatever...
I'll go in your place.
Sciurus, this isn't a game.
Why don't you take a look in the mirror before you say that crap, Ratatos?
You're exhausted, you look pale as a ghost. Hell, you look completely out of it.
How am I supposed to relax when I have someone like you there to save my husband?
I don't know what you and Enciodes talked about in that room.
But I'm not going to watch you hand the Browntail family over to him!
Why are you staring at me like that? You want a fight?
You might just be right.
Fine, Doctor of Rhodes Island. Sciurus will help you with your plan in my place.
Though, to tell you the truth, I'm not holding out hope that we can get anything back from Enciodes.
All I can say is: good luck.
Why can't I go out, Uncle?
By the president's orders, you are not to go running around until he returns.
So when's he coming back?
The president is currently making his way to Karlan, and will be back in two days' time.
Look, I just want to catch a breath of fresh air, okay?
If you'll stay around the estate... I'll send someone to follow you.
Ensia, this is for your own good. The situation in Kjerag is precarious right now.
*Sigh*...
Do you really need to do all this... Enciodes?
Doctor, what should I do...?
Aurora?!
Shhh–– Keep it down, it was really hard for me to sneak in with all the guards.
Weren't you and Sharp going to stay with the Doctor?
Yes. I'm here because the Doctor wanted me to tell you something.
You're absolutely sure?
Yes, Arctosz has assembled a large number of soldiers in his territory, but he's not the one leading them – the Doctor is.
Where's Arctosz?
I'm not sure... From eyewitness accounts, he disappeared after formally handing leadership of his forces to the Doctor.
Is that Doctor's squad the bait? No, it can't be that simple.
How about their numbers?
The Paleroche clansmen who responded to Arctosz's call were mostly what you expected. Based on our estimates, Arctosz shouldn't have enough men to assemble another squad.
But there's no chance he's sitting in the back. There's a high possibility of there being another team.
The Paleroche clan's warriors are skilled at operating in the mountains. It's not beyond the realm of possibility.
They can't leave this squad alone though, regardless of whether it's a decoy or the main force.
Let Weiss pass on the orders and have the troops gather at the base of the mountain, awaiting further instructions.
Then dispatch someone to keep track of the army. Your primary responsibility is to find Arctosz.
On it.
The Doctor... wants me to climb up the mountain and sneak into the Vine-Bead Court to find my sister?
Correct.
Because conflict between the Paleroche and Silverash clans is almost unavoidable now.
But if there's one person who can put a stop to all of this, it's your sister, the Saintess, Enya Silverash.
But... But...
The Doctor wanted to stick to Rhodes Island's policy of nonintervention and stand by.
But since restoring the Saintess to power was just a ploy by Mr. Enciodes, the Doctor believes he doesn't care much for Kjerag's faith.
And once the Silverash clan's forces beat down the Paleroche clan's, the only thing stopping him from taking all of Kjerag is the Vine-Bead Court.
In other words, the object of faith for all the people of Kjerag – the Saintess.
The Doctor doesn't actually need you to convince the Saintess to do anything. The basic idea is there might be a way to heal the relationship between your brother and sister.
There might also be some guilt in there for not being able to help since coming to Kjerag.

I'd never blame the Doctor for that! I just want to apologize, both to the Doctor and everyone else who came with me. This was supposed to be a nice vacation, but my brother went and screwed everything up...
It's not your fault either, Cliffheart.
Anyway, the Doctor's been dragged into Enciodes's business, and can't stand on the sidelines anymore.
And anyway, you're Enciodes's sister. Nothing can change that.
So the Doctor's decided to make a move.
And it's to have you go up the mountain, and become a kind of insurance beside the Saintess.
The Doctor wants me to stop my brother's plans when the time comes.
Or, take the Saintess and run when the time comes.
Ensia subconsciously rubs the coil of rope in her hand.
Her sister braided it for her when she was a child.
I'll go.
This is a covert operation. I'll be there with the other operators to help.
I have a better idea.
Just get me to the base of Mount Karlan. I'll handle the rest myself.
Doctor, why are you marching us at such a slow pace?

opts: So as to give those prying eyes their fill.
We can't go any faster. Not right now.

lines.x: Gulo, don't tell me you still haven't figured out what we're doing?
Aren't we drawing the attention of the Silverash clan? I think we're going a bit too slow for that.
I see you're eager to just rush in and fight the Silverashes.
Sure am.
Last time, Valais handed me my ass, so I've got a chip on my shoulder.
Doctor, we've confirmed the location of Yucatan, who was taken away during the ceremony.
As you suspected, she was not taken to the Silverash clan's territory, but locked up at the base of the mountain.

opts: Good work.

lines.x: Doctor, you really are something else. You managed to find Old Yucatan this quickly.
Hmph. Such simple intelligence gathering is child's play for a Browntail.
Anyway, we've found Yucatan, so it's time for me and this idiot here to get moving, right?

opts: Right.

lines.x: What's with the silent treatment?
What, were you talking to me? I don't remember my folks ever calling me 'Idiot.'
(Sighs deeply)
General! Gulo! Hmph... You happy now?
I'm asking you, do you know what you're supposed to be doing?
Of course. Head to the nearest station, cause some chaos, and give you a chance to rescue your man.
Good.
But a word of warning from the Doctor –– don't actually kill anyone.
Hmph. The station's full of civilians. I swear on my name not to lay a finger on them.
But, Doctor, if the Silverashes show up...
All I can say is, I'll try to hold back.
Cliffheart, did you say you were looking for... your mountaineering equipment?
Yup.
Don't tell me you're going to climb Mount Karlan...
Eh... From what you've told me, there should be a ton of people in Karlan.
If we're talking about heading up the face of the mountain, even the squad leader would have some difficulty sneaking in.
But that's nothing I can't handle~
As long as you can get me to the foot of the mountain, I can handle the rest.
But... the others and I won't be able to protect you.
No biggie, no biggie.
I've gotten plenty of training at Rhodes Island, so I'm more than capable of handling myself in an ordinary fight.
But...
Ensia, where are you going?
Uncle Chester...
I want to go climbing.
Which mountain are we talking here?
Mount Karlan.
That place is going to turn into a battlefield. You'll get hurt if you go.
Why do you want to go there?
Uncle Chester, ever since I fell in love with mountaineering, I've always wanted to summit Mount Karlan with my own two hands.
I've only wanted it even more since my sister left us and became the Saintess... One day, I would reach the peak and bring my sister home.
And now, my chance has finally come.
You won't be able to do that, Ensia.
Even if I can't bring my sister back home, I need to be with her at a time like this.
She must be very confused after everything our brother's done.
If I can't stop him, then, I at least want to be there for her!
Olafur, your youngest daughter's gotten pretty big too.
Young Lady, I am sorry, but by the Master's orders, you are not allowed to leave the estate. It's for your own safety.
Stand down.
Huh?
Uncle Chester!
Sir, you're directly contradicting the Master's orders!
I'll accept all responsibility for what happens here.
But... Even if you'll go so far to vouch for her, how can that be more important than her safety?
A Silverash never takes unnecessary risks.
This time, let's trust her.
Ugh... Fine.
Go, prepare a burdenbeast for the Young Lady and our guests from Rhodes Island.
Understood.
Thank you, Uncle Chester!
Thank you very much, Sir.
I always thought you...
No need to thank me.
Now go. Time is of the essence.
Okay!
Ensia.
Hm?
Be careful out there.
Will do!
Olafur.
Ever since you and Elizabeth passed on, I've always regretted that I stood against you and the reforms you tried to bring to our land. I should have stood with you.
So, when your son decided to take up your ideals, I chose to support him wholeheartedly.
Enciodes has done exceptionally well.
As the Saintess, Enya has won the adoration of the people of Kjerag.
Though Ensia contracted Oripathy, her condition is under control and she is as happy-go-lucky as always.
But, it suddenly just occurred to me.
How long has it been since I saw these three kids getting along, smiling and laughing as siblings should?
Great Elder, you still need to rest...

According to the records, this great hall was built by the people of all three clans following the establishment of the Tri-Clan Council. The existence and cooperation of the three clans far predates the modern Tri-Clan system.
Then, after thousands of years passed, the system was tweaked and sanded down, until it became what we see today.
And this chair may well have been here for thousands of years.
Over a dozen Great Elders sat on this seat, spending their lives here.
Juast as a dozen Saintesses found their final resting spot atop this mountain.
From the very beginning, the Saintess was a fabrication of the Vine-Bead Court to facilitate the spread of the faith.
Now that you've chosen to tell me these things I've known for a long time, are you meaning to say that our faith is false?
You seem to be a lot more direct as well. But I mean the opposite, Enya. Sorry, Saintess.
Saintess... *cough* *cough*... Do you know my original name?
I'm afraid no one in the present Vine-Bead Court remembers either.
Even I, myself, have forgotten.
Each Great Elder, after taking up the mantle of the previous one, is known only as 'Great Elder'.
One day, the people will also forget your name, and know you only as 'Saintess'.
Then, the Great Elder and Saintess come to realize––
What people believe in... is not Kjeragandr.
People can believe in anything, as long as there's a story, a few neat coincidences. They will believe in you, in me, in Enciodes, in the mountain, in the water, even in a single leaf.
If people can believe in anything, then what is faith?
The Great Elders finally came to an answer:
What people believe is faith itself.
People choose to vest power in the object of their faith.
People attribute the things that they shun to their faith.
People unconditionally trust the way of life to which their faith guides them.
So, what is faith?
Faith is laziness, escapism, and decadence! Faith is stability, faith is stagnation! *cough* *cough* *cough*––
Let's get you to bed, Great Elder.
No, Saintess. Saintess!
The Great Elder desperately clutches at Enya's wrist.
His grasp is so tight that it leaves a red mark on her skin, as if he spent all his remaining strength on it.
His eyes are fixed on the Saintess before him, but he seems to be looking at someone else.
Faith is ugly! But even so, it is indestructible!
In the thousands of years of Kjerag's history, the cracks between the three major clans have gradually widened, yet no one can deny faith. Faith is the foundation of Kjerag!
Faith unites the people of Kjerag. People seek out faith, people rely on faith!
Kjerag has existed like this for thousands of years!
The people crave stability! The people crave stagnation!
Enciodes thinks he has won, but he hasn't. He cannot win.
How could he possibly defeat the faith that has coalesced in Kjerag for millennia?
This old man is about to die, but you are still young. You are the Saintess of this Vine-Bead Court, and you will become the Great Elder of this Vine-Bead Court.
Go forth and instruct him, overcome him. Make him understand that in the face of faith itself, his actions are insignificant, beneath even mention!
Enya remains silent for a while, but forces herself free of the Great Elder's grip.
She feels pity for the old man before her, but she feels a greater determination.
...I disagree, Great Elder.
You say that faith equals stagnation, and that people depend on faith because they demand stability.
But that's not how I see things.
It is true that once people accept a faith, they come to rely on it. Laziness, escapism, decadence... I can't argue those.
But this does not mean that faith itself is stagnation.
Faith is just faith, nothing more. It has no attributes by itself, and its substance comes from what is put into it.
If the faith progresses, then the faithful will progress with it.
If the faith stagnates, then the faithful will stagnate with it.
The reason why you think the people of Kjerag crave stagnation is precisely because Kjeragandr's faith has been stagnant for thousands of years in the hands of the Vine-Bead Court!

We've drawn up this land as a prison to preserve the rules of some ancient agreement, surviving in these snow-capped mountains for millennia without exploring or even making contact with the outside world.
And you mean to tell me this agreement has anything to do with our faith?
Is it not just the arrogance of the Vine-Bead Court?
Arrogance? No, no, this is how things were meant to be!
Nothing is inherently meant to be.
If it seems that way, it's only because it's never been changed.
It's time to change, Great Elder.
No, Saintess, you cannot change anything.
Faith... *cough* *cough*... is the fundamental reason why Kjerag is Kjerag.
I will go and face Enciodes, but not in the way you want.
I don't know if I'll succeed.
But, since the Saintess has always been used by the Vine-Bead Court as a tool to guide the peoples' faith...
I shall put this tool to good use.
I am the Saintess, but I am not the Saintess of the Vine-Bead Court. I am the Saintess of Kjerag.
I will use my own methods to guide the people to freedom. The freedom to progress, to explore, to take risks.
And as for you, Great Elder, it is time to rest.
Attendants, come help the Great Elder away.
Enya stands up and walks to the door of the great hall, her eyes fixed forward.
Behind her, the Great Elder occupies his same seat, watching her back grow ever smaller in the distance. He stretches out his hand as to grasp something, but finally, powerlessly, it drops.
He grasps nothing at all.
He remains there evermore, motionless, just like the snow of Kjerag blanketing his dreams.
Sir, I've searched the woodlands around the perimeter of the Paleroche clan and found no signs of mobilization.
Keep looking.
Yessir.
Gnosis, we've got an issue.
Gulo split from the Doctor's forces and led a team around Mount Karlan, breaking through the pass and occupying the station there!
What?!
Right now, Gulo is driving everyone out of the station, and another team is heading for Klin.
That's where Yucatan is being held... Is this a rescue operation?
Impossible. They're being far too inefficient for that. There must be another motive.
Monch, based on the route you saw them taking, where do you think the Doctor is headed?
...They chose a route that could lead either to Mount Karlan or to the pass.
Oh... Could the Doctor intend to bypass Mount Karlan to directly attack our territory?
I should've warned you about this possibility.
No, I was caught off guard myself. I should've stationed more troops at the pass.
The Doctor...
But, Arctosz still hasn't shown himself, and we cannot say for sure what their true objective is...
Grr, I'll have Valais head to the station to deal with Gulo, then Weiss will go to Klin.
Have a portion of the troops stationed at the base of the mountain go defend the pass and prevent the Doctor's forces from breaking through to our territory.
Understood.
General, we've already taken control of the station. Why are we still waiting here?
The Doctor told us to avoid hurting anyone, but I didn't hear anything about not smashing the place up. This station is a symbol of Silverash treachery.
Calm down. We'll see when the next train arrives––
Grr... When's the thing supposed to arrive anyway? Let me see the note the Doctor left me...
Okay, that sounds about right.
Huh?! What's going on here?!
This station now belongs to Gulo of the Paleroche clan! Get out of here, if you value your life!
Why is the Paleroche clan here?!
And it's none other than the terrible Gulo! Did the Paleroches declare war?!
Ugh... I'm too young to die! Run!
That's right! Run, faster!
Umm... General Gulo, thanks for bringing me along.
Hmph. I would want nothing to do with a Silverash daughter, if not for the fact that the Doctor told me you were helping.
You really aren't in cahoots with that brother of yours?
I'm here to stop him.
...I don't trust you, but I do trust the Doctor. The one called Sharp told me to trust in the victory the Doctor would bring.
Alright. Didn't you say you had to make your way up the mountain? Off with you.
After you go, I'll have to halt the railroad... to do what again? Oh, right, gotta stop things from running so that Silverash reinforcements don't show up too quickly.
Well, thanks anyway, General Gulo!
These people make no sense to me at all.
Whatever. I have my own things to do anyway.
Hmph, that musclehead might not be the sharpest tool in the shed, but he certainly does know how to fight.
He should be fine holding down the station.
Next up is... Dammit, my ass..
Madame, why don't you get off the burdenbeast and rest for a bit? The rest of us can handle things.
Hmph. I don't have the luxury for that right now.
Our task is much more difficult than Gulo's.
In addition to rescuing Yucatan, we also have to take as much of Klin as we can.
Until the Doctor wins over on the other end, we have to draw the Silverashes' attention.
Madame, can we really hold out against those numbers with the forces we have here? Are you sure this Doctor isn't just using us as fodder?
That's right, the Matriarch...
Shut it.
Listen, Ratatos is sick and tired of carrying this clan on her back, so she's trusting this to me.
We're at a do or die moment for the house of Browntail. If we succeed, the Browntail clan might just have a future. If we fail, we're all changing our names to Silverash.
I don't care what Enciodes is trying to do or what he wants Kjerag to be, but us Browntails will never be anyone's fodder.
If you're that eager to get a new name, then you can leave now. But if being a Browntail means anything to you, then come with me!
(I've never seen Sciurus so charismatic before...)
(I didn't know Sciurus had such a talent for speeches...)
(I think she really is speaking her mind. She's just never been given a chance before.)
What are you mumbling about over there?!
(Even though I heard that the Matriarch herself was planning on surrendering, if Sciurus isn't willing to give up, then...)
(It would be really shameful for us to raise the white flag.)
(That's right. And to be honest, I find it hard to believe that the Matriarch would do something like that...)
You're still not done? If you don't like it here, then beat it!
We fight for the house of Browntail!
Hmph, that's more like it.
Alright, I'll handle the rest.
Are you serious about this?
Don't worry. I'm a bona-fide pro.
You'd better not die on us.
I'll be waiting for the good news.
As much as I've trained, it's been a long time since I've done any real mountaineering. Not since I came to Rhodes Island for treatment.
This sure is getting my blood pumping, haha...
Wait for me, sis. I'm coming for you.
Ensia remembers how when she was a child, she always loved bringing her sister along to her climbs.
Though she was physically fit, she'd always give up halfway through.
Her favorite thing to do was to curl up on the sofa by the fire, knitting something for herself or her brother, or just napping.
'My sister probably doesn't like the mountains.' That's what Ensia thought.
But in the end, after becoming the Saintess, she was bound atop the mountain.
Ensia always had a dream – that one day, she would save her sister from the mountain.
General, this railway should be useless now, right?
No clue. But it probably isn't gonna work anymore.
But who gives a shit. The other job the Doctor gave us was to start some chaos in Silverash territory, the bigger the better.
Since we've already messed this place up pretty good, let's go somewhere else––
...Huh?
You sure took your sweet time, Valais.
Gulo, what exactly is the Doctor after?
The sentimental side of me actually wants to tell you.
But, honestly, I got no clue what the Doctor is trying to do either, hahaha!
But what I do know is, I'm here to make enough trouble to give Enciodes a tough time!
Gulo, I'd prefer not to humiliate you.
Or any of the excellent warriors of the Paleroche clan.
So lay down your arms. You have no chance of winning.
Valais, I know you've got some bad blood, and I'm not good at comforting people.
I also know that there's nothing I can do to bring back what you've lost.
But I'm happy to rumble with you. Let you blow off your steam.
Phew... That should be it for the first checkpoint.
Ah... If I remember right, this trail was part of the trial to become a Saintess.
Ensia remembers, the night before the selection of the Saintess, her sister had some kind of fight with her brother.
That night, she sat alone on the sofa in the living room until the sun came up.
She still remembered telling her sister that if not for the fact that she was too young, she would've joined the Saintess selection process herself. Her sister smacked her upside the head.
Her sister was quite strong.
But never did she expect that this would be the last time her sister would smack her around.
Sir, it's starting to snow.
A good opportunity.
Kjeragandr smiles upon us, by the look of things.
Though it took a lot of extra time to make our way to the Holy Hunt, it was well worth it.
How's the situation at the foot of the mountain?
It seems like the Doctor was able to divert their attention. The forces stationed there were far fewer than we expected.
Haha, good! Very good! The Doctor is quite capable!
Still, we can't afford to bring shame to the Paleroche name.
Warriors, now that our time is nigh, we need no longer conceal ourselves.
Forget the Silverashes! Follow me – we'll rush up the mountain and rescue the Saintess!
Yessir!
Why'd it have to start snowing now...?
Old Kjeragandr really has a dry sense of humor.
Or, maybe this is a test for me, as the sister of the Saintess?
Well, whatever.
A bit of wind and snow can't hold me back!
You can do this, Ensia! You weren't just talking big in front of Aurora! You've got this!
It was hard to send letters back to Kjerag from Rhodes Island.
Usually, she would only get the opportunity when Courier showed up.
Each time he visited, she always felt conflicted.
Fortunately, Courier would bring a letter from her sister.
Unfortunately, Courier and Matterhorn would always treat her like a kid, as if she couldn't see the sadness on their faces.
She is also a child of the Silverash family. She read the books left behind by her parents and knew of the vision they had for Kjerag. She also got good grades in Hypatia's class!
Of course she knew.
What she did not know is... Did things have to turn out like this?
If so, then why her family?
Sir, troops led by Arctosz suddenly appeared at the foot of the mountain and broke through the defensive line!
Grr... Where'd they come from?
I'm afraid they entered the site of the Holy Hunt, so our men were unable to track them down...
And our forces stationed there couldn't stop them?
A portion of the forces were split off to defend the pass, plus it suddenly started snowing, and morale was low to begin with. Since their goal was to break through the line...
You've been outplayed, Gnosis.
You made the right call. They care only about Mount Karlan and the Saintess. Everything else is secondary to that.
Did you come back only to mock me?
No, I would've hesitated too, in your position.
Just as you used your longstanding reputation within Karlan Trade to put on a play of half-truths...
Our lack of familiarity with the Doctor worked to that one's advantage – no shortage of tricks up those mysterious sleeves.
You may believe that the Doctor has designs on Kjerag, but from my perspective, I don't necessarily think that the Saintess is the only objective.
Of course, the Doctor could not be sure that we would see things this way, and that was the wager.
That you'd see the Doctor's actions around the pass as a move against Kjerag.
The Doctor only required that you be sufficiently concerned about this possibility to send your troops to assuage those concerns.
As such, the Doctor won the bet.
Enciodes, your arrogance is truly loathsome. To wax admiringly about the actions of your enemy at a time like this.
This is not a game, and we cannot afford to fail.
I am not a fan of failure, Gnosis.
I've already sent Degenbrecher.
To intercept Arctosz?
No... Why do you think the Doctor chose a route that could lead either to Mount Karlan or the pass?
You're saying that Arctosz's forces are a ruse.
From the very beginning, the Doctor's forces were both the bait and the main force.
No wonder why they were moving so slowly. They were waiting.
That's right. And, if I'm not mistaken, this is when they will start making their move.
If the Doctor is allowed to group with Arctosz, then there would be real trouble.
Ensia already knows.
She knows her wish cannot come true.
She cannot save her sister from the mountain.
She also cannot mend the relationship between her two siblings.
How could she have no idea as to what her brother seeks to accomplish?
How could she not see that the rift between the two was only growing wider and wider?
But what can she do?
She doesn't like to cry, to vent her frustrations to others, or to give up.
All she can do is wedge the climbing pick in her hand into the rock above her head, and momentarily forget some of her troubles as she overlooks the scenery from atop the mountain.
Drifting snow strikes her face, leaving traces that look like streaks of tears.
Doctor, Master Arctosz has begun to move.
We've also heard that the Silverashes are reinforcing the pass.
So far, everything is proceeding as you have planned.
So she's here.
This is as far as you go, Rhodes Islander.
Hm?
I remember your codename. You're Sharp.
Lucky the Doctor brought some spare clothes.
So that's what's going on. This entire army is both the genuine article and also a decoy.
To be precise, this plan was specially tailored to you.
The Doctor had to plan for a contingency that included a woman strong enough to tear any plan to shreds.
I am not Enciodes. The admiration and respect of my enemies mean nothing to me.
The only thing I need of my enemies is for them to be strewn across the ground.
Then you're in luck. You've got a whole army of opponents right in front of you.
You're the only one in this crowd worthy of being my opponent.
Degenbrecher shakes her head, sighing as if offering an immutable truth within her opinion.
Sharp looks at the woman before him, and for some reason, is reminded of his friend Stormeye, a fellow elite operator.
Stormeye would've been very happy to face an opponent like her.
But to him, whether mingling with the army here, or dealing with Degenbrecher, all of this is just a part of the job.
It's a shame, an enemy's respect doesn't do much for me either.
I dislike glory, but you don't seem to care about it at all.
Nothing personal. It's just business.
Just business?
Doing business requires professionalism. Only an amateur would bring personal feelings to the job.
There's nothing worth bragging about when it comes to my combat abilities. It's just one of my tools to get the job done.
Hahaha, I like the way you think.
I've always despised self-righteous challengers, pumped up on their skills or ideals.
And, without exception, I crushed them all underfoot.
I have heard of your feats as The Black Knight.
A Leithanian who cannot use Arts, rendering all dignity and pride worthless before your greatsword.
Where is that greatsword, by the way?
Waving something like that around in this unsophisticated place would only scare the natives, so I decided to switch weapons.
Not that it matters what I use, though.
Degenbrecher flourishes the blade in her hand, and in the blink of an eye, the wind and snow itself seem to cleave in two.
As for me, I was promised victory.
Sharp bends forward slightly, placing his hand on the hilt of the standard-issue knife at his waist.
Degenbrecher notices a familiar scent from him.
Back when she was in Kazimierz, only a few people gave off this same scent.
The scent of war.
Do you come from the Court of Sargon?
That was my previous employment.
Never thought you'd be able to identify Sargon swordsmanship.
I have little interest in Arts. But when it comes to weaponry, I couldn't forget anything, even if I wanted to.
No wonder they called The Black Knight a natural-born warrior.
Natural-born?
Hmph.
Do you know how long it took to go from 'a Leithanian who can't use Arts' to 'a natural-born warrior?'

lines.st: 'Click.'
The shield in Sharp's hand reveals a crack.
Though there are several wounds on Degenbrecher's body, she seems not to notice them.

lines.x: Do you have no sense of pain?
If growing up, the only waking sensation you knew was pain, then you too would be used to it.
How about you? How many times have you danced with death?
Everyone I knew who even thought of counting didn't survive long in battle.

I heard that The Black Knight, despite being a three-time champion, was exiled from Kazimierz and hunted for refusing to work with the General Chamber of Commerce, eventually disappearing beyond Kazimierz's borders.
So?
Once a symbol of refusal to bow to authority, now a conspirator's bodyguard taking part in the usurpation of a country.
I don't pass any moral judgments. I just can't help but to feel a bit let down.
I thought a Rhodes Islander of all people would understand Enciodes's ideas.
Understanding is one thing. Acceptance is another.
Actually, I think the Doctor understands Enciodes well.
I'm not going to defend Enciodes or myself. It's too much of a hassle, and also unnecessary.
See for yourself what he wants with your own eyes – if you survive, that is.
After a great many struggles, Ensia finally reaches the edge of the cliff. She can scale it easily, but the sound of a nearby conversation makes her freeze.
Everything's gone to shit down there, and everything's gone to shit up here too.
Yeah. I can't believe the Great Elder went down like that, and now Arctosz is rampaging his way up here.
I was under the assumption that the worst was over, but now I have no idea what's going to happen next.
I didn't really think there'd be peace anyway, but now that things have gone south, I feel a little guilty.
Guilty? Why?
Since we entered the Vine-Bead Court, the Lady–– Err, the Saintess, has clearly taken the reins.
Since the Great Elder collapsed, the entire Vine-Bead Court has fallen in line with the Saintess.
And below the mountain, Sir Enciodes has taken complete control.
When you put it that way, it all makes sense, huh. The Saintess controls the mountain, and Sir Enciodes controls everything below.
Borrowing your words, I have no idea what's going to happen next.
We're just underlings anyway. There's no point thinking too hard about it.
Yeah, you're right. Why even bother?
Let's make two more rounds. Maybe we'll get called down the mountain by then.
Sis...
Hey! What's that?!
The granary is on fire!
What?! Someone go put it out, hurry!
Heh, piece of cake.
Madame, you certainly have a unique way of doing things, though that's to be expected...
The hell are you trying to say?!
N-Nothing, it was a compliment.
Hmph. I'll pay for them to rebuild the granary after we're all done here, but for now, we're on urgent business!
Stop dragging your feet! The fire's got their attention, so go rescue Yucatan! The place is right ahead!
Yes, Madame!
...Kjarr.
I'm here, Saintess.
During the ceremony, I recall that when I happened to spot you in the crowd, there was a hooded figure next to you.
That's right.
I've heard that this person is a guest from Rhodes Island invited by Enciodes. A guest known as the Doctor.
But now, that individual stands with Arctosz, fighting against Enciodes.
...Nothing escapes you, Saintess.
How did you come to know this person?
By chance, when I was heading down the mountain to deliver your letter.
...I'll take your word for it then.
Why did this question suddenly grip your thoughts?
I would like to meet this person.
Huh?
Though I don't know exactly why, the Doctor stands in direct opposition to Enciodes.
I want to know exactly why.
I no longer doubt what I must do, but I am afraid that I made up my mind too late.
There are some things I must fight for.
About that... You needn't worry yourself.
Why not?
Because the Doctor's emissary is right behind you.
Huh?!
Sis!
Ensia... I suppose I've finally started hallucinating.
Snap out of it, sis! You're not hallucinating! It's me, your sister, Ensia!
What?! Ensia, is that really you?
It is! Here, come touch me if you don't believe it.
Enya wants to reach out with her shaking hands to feel Ensia.
She fears that the sister who suddenly appeared before her is an illusion.
But before she has a chance to do so, Ensia has already plunged into her arms.
She knows right away, without a shadow of a doubt, that the woman embracing her is her dearest little sister.
It's you, Ensia... It really is you...
I've missed you so much.
I've missed you too.
Have you been well?
What of your illness?
What kind of place is this Rhodes Island?
You're so silly. You already asked all these things in your letters.
I have to tell you...
Sharp takes a few steps back with a shallow breath.
He has fought many opponents throughout his career, and has also learned from – or at least watched – many formidable fighters at Rhodes Island.
But he has to admit – he has never faced anyone like Degenbrecher before. Or rather, an opponent as pure and simple as Degenbrecher.
She has speed and strength, qualities that every warrior should possess.
Degenbrecher has nothing more, but clearly needs nothing more.
Why would anyone need anything more than incredible speed and strength?
Fortunately... Sharp became an elite operator because he also possesses these two things.
You have a lot of trust in Enciodes.
What makes you say that?
Few people have the capacity to do this for money.
And besides, you are not a person who can be won over with money.
What about you? You said this is just business, but what you've done goes well beyond duty.
I assure you, the work I do is the work I like.
...I didn't like Kazimierz. Too chaotic, and the people are too loud.
I thought I wouldn't like Kjerag either.
But Kjerag isn't too bad.
That's a fair reason. Having a nice place to retire is important.
As if reaching a tacit agreement, the two sides take a few steps back.
They both realize that their previous moves achieved nothing toward the goal of eliminating the opponent.
They would have to tap into more of their power.
Degenbrecher casts her sword aside, and pulls the swordbreakers from her waist.
Sharp likewise discards his shield and holds his knife with both hands.
An evergreen tree branch buckles under the weight of the snow with a crackling sound.
In the same instant, Sharp and Degenbrecher both vanish from where they were just a second ago.
Honestly, what am I going to do with you?
You actually managed to sneak past everyone and climb all the way up from the foot of the mountain.
Heheh, it was the only way for me to see you.
You were just saying a certain 'the Doctor' sent you up here?
To what end?
The Doctor told me I should stay by your side, protect you when you need it, or help you escape.
When I need it?
Yeah, like if our brother tries to do something bad to you.
Okay, this is what I think.
He has his ideas, and I can't stop him.
But if those ideas end up putting you in danger...
I'll put my life on the line to stop him.
Enya suddenly understands why the Doctor sent her sister to her side.
For protection. For a way out. These are the two reasons told to Ensia.
But the Doctor also knows about her ideals, as well as the fact that being able to see her sister would be inherently meaningful.
Have Ensia protect her?
Have Ensia help her escape?
No, quite the opposite.
She is the one who should protect Ensia.
She is the one who should help Ensia escape.
She is the one –– who should be fighting.
Ensia, I see you're still wearing the bracelet I made for you.
Of course. And I'll keep wearing it.
I'll find the time to make a new one for you.
Really?!
Mmhmm.
So, Ensia...
Yeah?
Do you want to do a little favor for me?
A favor?
Come with me.
(It's awfully quiet outside, but there should still be two guards.)
(The window... If I don't want them to see me from this angle, I have to lead the guards away first.)
(The patrol route... They pass through this area five times a day, and should be coming around soon.)
(No, I can't force open the lock... But if I can pick it...)
(...Then there might still be a chance.)
Sir Enciodes... This time I swear...
(––They're coming!)
The conflict... three clans seems inevitable, but...
...Browntail clan's... burning... Sciurus...
(Sciurus...?!)
(Why did they mention Rus? Did something happen to her?!)
(Dammit, I'm still too far away... I can't hear clearly.)
(No, she can handle herself. I have to find a way out of here first, then find Sciurus and the Matriarch...)
Halt, who are you people?!
Stop them right now–– Guh...
(There's a commotion coming from the entrance... What happened?)
Yucatan, are you alright?!
Rus?! What are you doing here?
Huh? Are you dumb? I'm here for you, obviously!
Everyone else is so worthless, I had to rescue you myself!
But, it's too dangerous!
Rus, how many did you bring with you? Y-You didn't have to come yourself, you know. I wouldn't want anything to happen to you!
Yucatan.
Say one more word and I'm going to beat the crap out of you.
Umm...
Shut up! Think about what you'd do for me. I'll do just the same for you!
And you'd better not forget it!
Also, you're not allowed to abandon me ever again.
Rus...
You know I'd never do that.
Hmph! Of course I know!
Alright, the fact that you're okay is a huge load off my shoulders... Now, I have something else to take care of. I promised Ratatos I'd finish another job after I save you.
A job?
Madame Sciurus, Weiss of the Silverash clan is moving to intercept us!
Right on schedule. I'll tell you the details later, but our job is to cause some trouble for those Silverash bastards.
And... there's someone else I want to talk to. But that comes later!
Things aren't looking too good, and Weiss won't go down easy... But we're in this together. Right, Yucatan?
Of course. I'll be there for you as long as you need me.
Close in on them!
Don't let Arctosz up the mountain path!
Sir, we're about to be completely encircled!
Hmph... I suppose this is as far as we could get just charging through.
Please lay down your arms, Arctosz.
Child of Yek, I don't know what Enciodes offered you to make you so infatuated with him.
The Yek family is prominent in Paleroche lands.
Yet you still chose to help Enciodes usurp Kjerag!
Lay down my arms?
Never.
Arrest Arctosz... But try to take him alive.
Come at me, Silverash scum! Who's brave enough to face me?!
...Aurora?!
Does that mean––

opts: Looks like I caught up to you.
Hey.
(Tug on the brim of your hood)

lines.x: Doctor––?!
Don't tell me, Degenbrecher...
Ah, Doctor, it's about damned time!

opts: The snow delayed things a bit.
Avoiding Silverash eyes and ears took time.

lines.x: Haha, Kjerag's snow is a challenge for outsiders like you.
No worries. All that matters is that you're here!
Heh, now that reinforcements are here, nothing can stop us!
Isn't it too early to celebrate, Arctosz?
Enciodes!
Long time no see, Doctor.

opts: 'Long time no see' indeed.
Has it really been that long?
So, you're finally willing to meet me.

lines.x: Though there's a lot I want to talk to you about, now is not the time, unfortunately.
Just you wait, Enciodes. I'll break through your encirclement, rescue the Saintess, and prove to all of Kjerag that you're the real traitor!
I’m looking forward to it.
Warriors, what has been the house of Paleroche's duty since the beginning of time?
'Defend Kjerag from foreign enemies, and protect the peace of the land!'
Now, Enciodes seeks to work against the Saintess and use her as his tool.
Can we, the hosue of Paleroche, accept that?
'No! No! Absolutely not!!!'
Reinforcements have arrived. Follow me up Mount Karlan, topple Enciodes, and rescue the Saintess!
'Topple Enciodes, rescue the Saintess!'
'Topple Enciodes, rescue the Saintess!!'
'Topple Enciodes, rescue the Saintess!!!'
Saintess, what are you doing?!
I'm heading down the mountain.
Sir Enciodes said––
Since when do I need Enciodes's consent to do things?
'Ding ding', 'ding ding', 'ding ding––'
An otherworldly bell sounds throughout the court.
The next moment, everyone present except Ensia falls to the ground.
Wow... incredible...
Kjarr, Kjarr.
Saintess, how did you know I was here?
I can guess where a clever girl hides.
I need to make a trip down the mountain, so please look after things here.
Oh, are you leaving the hard work to me?
There are even more troublesome things waiting for me.
If that's the case, I suppose I can't object.
In fact, during each Saintess selection, I'd dress up as a maid and secretly observe the girls being tested.
You were the sixth one that year.
I didn't have very high expectations for you, at first, since the Saintesses have been more and more boring as of late.
They may have been strong of body, but none of them were sufficiently strong of will and all ended up puppets of the Elders.
I sometimes wonder if this is because Kjerag is too closed-off. They, and the people of this country too, stagnated.
But you're different.
You're very interesting, Enya.
You didn't come here because you wanted to be the Saintess, but because of your anger and disappointment.
You faced the unforgiving elements on the trail up the mountain, you refused to give up even when your body had reached its limits, the thing on your mind...
Was not becoming a Saintess, or becoming an envoy of Kjeragandr. Rather, you were thinking about how best to serve the Kjerag people.
You thought about how disappointed you were in your brother, how disgusted you were with the other two clans, and the rage you felt at becoming the Saintess.
But the real surprise was your attitude towards Kjerag.
Or more accurately, the surprise that your family brought me.
Your brother gave me his answer sheet very early on.
And you have been searching for your own answers this entire time.

Over the years, you've endured the indifference with which the Saintess is treated in the Vine-Bead Court, while actively searching for a way to help the people. These things have become your nourishment.
And now, all that nourishment has transformed into your strength. You have not lost yourself amidst the turmoil, and you have made your choice.
Congratulations, my child, my envoy, my friend, my sister.
Forgive me for not being able to guide you. I rashly branded this land with my mark, and still regret it to this day.
But at least, allow me to add some color to your journey.
Above the silent passageway, Kjarr smiles and gently pats her hands together.
'Clap, clap.'
After which, everything changes.
Just a scratch!
Ugh...
Valais, I'm not sure if Master Arctosz wronged you in some way––
But Enciodes isn't exactly a saint himself!
I already told you.
Kjerag needs change.
This is also so that excellent warriors like yourself will no longer make sacrifices in vain!
Don't force my hand, Valais.
That's my line, Gulo.
The two of them take a step back and assume a solid stance.
They know that today, one of them must fall.
What's that?
The dark cloud... cleared?
Gah... What a stubborn one. I underestimated you.
But, even if you manage to defeat us here, it won't make any difference.
You cannot stop the Master.
Like I care.
The Matriarch... Ratatos always had her own ideas about Kjerag.
She also wants people to move forward and hopes that everyone can have a better future.
Except, she doesn't have the power.
And so, she became Ratatos the schemer, as you people call her.
Maybe to you, my sister is just a bump in the road.
But to me, she'll always be my beloved sister!
Yucatan...
We don't hold any personal grudges against you. As long as you're willing to lay down your arms, we guarantee your safety.
I'm not going to let Rus and I become bargaining chips for Enciodes to use against the Matriarch.
Weiss of the Silverash clan, step aside, or I will kill you.
The same goes for you, Yucatan of the Browntail clan.
You must remain here.
You can do it, Yucatan!
Huh...?
Look, Mount Karlan! Are those dark clouds clearing up?!
Sharp turns and watches Degenbrecher slowly return her swordbreakers to her waist.
Around them, trees lie snapped in twain and rocks shattered as if a Catastrophe swept through the land.
He doesn't notice his knife slipping, because he has already lost feeling in his right hand.
He has lost.
Best not overuse your right hand for the next three months.
Thanks for the tip. Looks like it's time to take a brief medical leave.
You don't seem very upset about it.
The outcome of our fight here doesn't change anything.
I kept you occupied here until now, so I've done my job. The others will handle the rest.
Now that my job's done, it's time to clock out.
Is your job that important to you?
Maintaining professionalism is very important to me.
Finding a good employer isn't easy, so I do happen to care about this job.
What about you? You knew from the beginning that I was here to waste your time. You don't look that upset about it.
Call it a bit of curiosity. Even if you managed to delay me here, what exactly can you accomplish?
Looks like, Enciodes's bodyguards are good for doing more than just his dirty work.
People only know that Enciodes bought The Black Knight. They don't know the Black Knight also approves of Enciodes.
You and Enciodes are both prideful.
...I'll remember your name, Sharp.
You don't have to remember my name, but you would do well to remember the Doctor's.
Hm?
What's going on at Mount Karlan?
Soldiers, in formation.
Yessir!
All forces––
What happened?
Time seems to stand still in this moment.
Only the Saintess's footsteps reverberate through the mountains.
The wind and snow already gave way, at some unknown time.
The dark clouds overhead seem to part before her path, slowly spreading to both sides.
The light filtering through the clouds shines upon the Saintess, and for a second, the people see her descending from the sky itself.
But this is no illusion.
A miracle...
It's a miracle.
It's a miracle!!!
In this moment, only one thought remains in the hearts of the warriors.
And that is –– to offer their staunchest devotion to the Saintess.
Kjarr.
Go. I will attend to the Great Elder.
Of course.
Come to think of it, even though the great hall has been repaired several times, it may not be much younger than Her.
The same goes for the title of Great Elder.
No one would deny that the existence of the Vine-Bead Court has allowed Kjerag to remain united all these years.
But what was correct in the past may no longer be correct.
If you cannot adapt to the changing times, then no matter how strong you are, you are fated to be cast aside.
Kjerag should not suffer that fate. At least, that is what some people hope.
Sweet dreams, Great Elder.
You've done well. You've all done well.
...How?
...What is THAT?
Is that... a miracle...?
What's going on at Mount Karlan?
All forces, pull back.
What... just happened?
Can it be... a miracle from Kjeragandr?
What will happen to Kjerag now...?
Hurts my eyes.
You've got me wondering now. Is this also a part of your Doctor's plans?
I don't know myself.
To be honest, if this was a part of the Doctor's plans, or even an anticipated possibility...
Even if some operators act like the Doctor is omnipotent, I think this would be a bridge too far.
Enya, I'm scared...
Don't be, Ensia.
I will protect you.
I... will protect Kjerag.
All the warriors along the mountain trail lay their weapons down.
In the face of a miracle from the Goddess of the Snowy Mountains, the goddess they have worshipped all their lives, their mortal squabbles seem ridiculous.
I, leader of the Paleroche clan, Arctosz Paleroche, offer my most sincere blessings to Her envoy, the Saintess.
After a short silence, he slowly bows to the Saintess and kneels before her.
All the Paleroche warriors follow his lead, repeating his actions.
...Having second thoughts now, Enciodes?
What do you mean?
You ignored my advice and brought that Doctor to Kjerag.
Do you think we lost?
Does this not seem like a loss to you?
It might have, in the past.
But today... maybe not.
Enciodes steps forward.
He strides out from the crowd and also approaches the Saintess, taking a knee and offering his blessings.
All the warriors of the Silverash clan follow suit.
And thus, the Snowcap Incident comes to an unexpected conclusion.
Thousands of years ago, Kjeragandr descended into this land, naming it Kjerag and bestowing upon it Her eternal vigil.
For thousands of years, Her teachings have continued to pervade the lives of all of Kjerag's people.
She gave Her flesh to the Paleroche clan, Her hide to the Browntail clan, and Her bones to the Silverash clan.
But we all share a common name –– the people of Kjerag.
Our belief in Her is the foundation of Kjerag, and is also the reason why we stand here today and call ourselves the children of Kjerag.
We all harbor the greatest respect for Her in our hearts, and work together to survive in this land to this day.
However, as we all know now, Kjerag is not the only country in this world.
Beyond these snow-capped mountains lies a vast world filled with people who do not look like us, do not speak like us, and do not share our belief in Kjeragandr.
In the past, we've had terrible disagreements over how to deal with such people.
In reality, these disagreements are not an existential crisis. Rather, they are Her way of testing us, Her way of guiding us.
She is the object of faith for all the people of Kjerag, our mother and guardian.
And as a mother, she never wishes to see her children stagnate.
Therefore...
Do not let your desire to preserve the old prevent you from trying the new. You are Her children, and you should be full of courage.
Do not let your nostalgia for your homeland prevent you from exploring new places. No matter where you go, She will walk with you.
Do not let what you already possess prevent you from seeking greater ventures, for you have the right to more in life.
After all, She is tolerant. The land She protects should be tolerant, and Her people should also be tolerant.
By the authority She has vested in me, I solemnly declare:
The Vine-Bead Court will tolerate the beliefs of outsiders towards Kjeragandr.
Furthermore, the Vine-Bead Court encourages all citizens to pursue a better life in their own ways.
In the commemoration of the Saintess of Mount Karlan, the Illuminated One, known as the Blessed Enya Silverash, and the resounding speech she delivered to the people of Kjerag––
This day is officially designated a national religious day throughout the land.

opts: (Chess pieces shift on the board.)

lines.x: The incident in the valley began as just an idle move.
I needed someone to distract the Paleroche clan so I could dispatch supplies and personnel in secret.
If not you, it could've been anyone else.
I planned on apologizing to you after all was said and done.
But now, I should probably apologize for underestimating the impact you would have.
Enciodes speaks to himself while moving a chess piece.

opts: (Chess pieces shift on the board.)

lines.x: Gnosis is my good friend, as well as my collaborator.
He and I do not necessarily see eye to eye – he has his own ideas and methods.
But I understand him.
As long as we share the same purpose, he will not betray me, nor do I need to exchange any information with him while executing a plan.
I could sit back and let him employ all his resources in whatever way he deemed efficacious to stymieing the power of the Browntail clan.
Destroying the railroad tracks was just a clever trick. Gnosis gained Ratatos's trust, and also blocked access to spies and Messengers from other countries.
Enciodes looks you in the eye and makes another move.

opts: (Chess pieces shift on the board.)

lines.x: It's a draw.
As if foreshadowing the end result, Enciodes takes his hands off the pieces and spreads them out.
It means this round is over.
It would appear that my luck has been quite rotten lately.
I can't even win at chess anymore.

opts: But you didn't lose.

lines.x: Sometimes, a draw is a great victory. And other times, a draw is a crushing defeat.

opts: In your case, which one is it?

lines.x: In my case... anything other than a great victory is a crushing defeat.
But this time... is indeed an exception.
Without a doubt, I have won.
But what I didn't expect was that you opened up another possibility for my victory.

opts: Another possibility?
I didn't have the means to persuade the Saintess to come down from the mountain.
I just wanted to prevent the loss of life.

lines.x: Yes, another possibility.
I thought that outcome was impossible, but you managed to see through it and influence the situation even without knowing anything.
That doesn't change the fact that I won.
Perhaps it's been too long since I've had a worthy opponent, but such a close match truly made my heart swell.
The Saintess... does not need anyone making decisions for her.
But you nudged her at just the right moment, giving her a direction.
Furthermore, you set the stage for her.
And you would have me believe that you did all of this unintentionally?
You certainly achieved this objective.
You sought out Arctosz and Ratatos, made battle predictions for them preemptively, and provided a better plan to avoid war.
This very much fits your way of doing things, Doctor.
Even I could not predict that this situation could be resolved in such a peaceful manner.
Surely, you understand why I was so eager to unite Kjerag under one banner.

opts: External concerns.

lines.x: That's right.
I don't particularly hate the Vine-Bead Court, nor am I in any rush to eradicate religion from these lands.
Nor am I particularly dissatisfied with Arctosz's insularity or Ratatos's hesitation.
They don't know the things that happen outside, and thus will not hold the same views as me.
If I had enough time, I wouldn't mind spending five or ten years, or even longer, changing their minds and changing Kjerag in a gentler way.
But––
Kjerag doesn't have enough time. Even if we cannot be considered rich, there are bound to be neighboring countries eyeing this land undisturbed by Catastrophes.
I must push us forward, and if there are people who cannot accept that, then I can only deprive them of their right to object.
That is all.

opts: You don't need to convince me.
These things are none of my business.

lines.x: I am simply sharing some of my thoughts with people worthy of hearing them.
You saw these things as a bystander, which is the only reason I'm able to tell you this.
As far as I know, Arctosz will announce his responsibility for poisoning the Great Elder and resign as head of the clan.
As for why he decided to make this show of grace, he decided to make an oath with the Saintess so that the she would take control of the Paleroche lands.
Perhaps to cement the Saintess's status.
The Browntail clan has signed an agreement of subordination to the Silverash clan.
I can share some extra details with you. I have no desire to take over the Browntail clan; should Ratatos desire, her power would not be any less than it was in the past.
There are no longer any left who desire stagnation, who refuse to budge, or would fret over costs and benefits. None stand in the way of Karlan Trade and Kjerag's development.
This is an outcome I can accept, Doctor.

opts: You and the Saintess aren't so different.

lines.x: But we ended up walking separate paths.
And, this is the result of my past choices. I will take responsibility for it.
She, too, must bear the consequences of her choices, and lift the people of Kjerag out of this stagnation with her guidance, not my instigation.
You proved yourself to me and to Kjerag, but you also... proved Enya.
Mr. President, it's almost time for the meeting.
Of course.
Regardless, Kjerag is about to undergo massive changes, Doctor.
Next time, it may be difficult for me to meet with you.
But I can assure you that Rhodes Island is free to come and go anywhere in Kjerag.
Additionally, please accept this token of my goodwill as compensation for involving you and your operators in this incident.
Neither Rhodes Island's name nor yours will appear in any future official records related to this event.
Also, a new contract that will allow us to call each other allies is on its way to Rhodes Island.
After this is all over, I will visit Rhodes Island again.
And when that time comes, I hope to play another game of chess with you, Doctor.
Kjarr.
Hm?
You've got some guts, you know. How long have you been keeping me in the dark?!
Do forgive me, Saintess, for I know not how I have sinned.
Oh? So be it, then.
But, Kjarr, I do have something I want to ask you.
Yes, Saintess.
...I want to know, was it Her intention for me to become the leader of Kjerag?
Was all of this Her plan? Me feeling lost and confused throughout this entire chain of events, and then my determination when making that final choice––
Did She expect all of that?
Kjarr understands why Enya suddenly asks that question now, of all times.
She is afraid.
She is not afraid that she made the wrong decision. But, she is afraid that everything, as far as her eyes can see, is just a game played by gods.
There is but one thing I can assure you...
She... has never once sought to interfere with your free will. Your thoughts have always been your own.
Who is to say whether Her miracles are nothing more than the wishes and fabrications of Her faithful?
Perhaps, She gave everything so that the people of wind and snow would have a pristine land to call home.
She looks out over this land, searching for ways to bless the people who live here with happiness.
As for the answer you gave – She acknowledges it, nothing more.
If that's true, then can you do me a favor?
Name the favor.
Please ask Her not to take you away.
Isn't that what always happens in the stories? Though I've found my answer, it comes at the cost of losing you.
I will not ask favors of Her willy-nilly, so would you continue to be my maid?
...Pfft.
Haha... Hahaha.
Sorry, sorry. I didn't expect you to make such a cute request.
But... let's do this according to chapter three of the scriptures.
If one day, you no longer uphold the ideals you currently do, then I will no longer stay by your side. But, until then, I swear to serve as your head maid.
No taking that back, okay?
Okay.
You want me to be your representative to Rhodes Island?
That's right.
You can devote yourself to your research at Rhodes Island. I trust it'll be to your liking.
Did you already have that in mind when you invited them?
Just another possibility.
After all, regardless of whether or not our plans succeed, your situation in Kjerag is only going to get worse.
You just didn't expect that the person you were trying to win over the most would almost sabotage your plan.
That takes nothing from our victory.
Though if you want to stay in Kjerag, I can also arrange that for you.
Well, whatever.
...I don't mind the infamy, but I'd get annoyed if people kept coming by to throw rocks at my lab's windows.
Also, I'd like to meet this Doctor for myself.
Oh, are you interested?
As if. I simply want to see whether your Doctor, who has already left a mark on this land, will end up becoming our enemy.
And if not?
Then I'd also like to know–– what level of technical prowess a company that has succeed in suppressing Oripathy possesses.
Gnosis, that gleeful hunger on your face looks about as bad as what you accused me of.
Gnosis, your luggage has been prepared. It's time to go.
Of course.
Shall we leave now then?
Just a moment.
Monch!
Nobody responds to Gnosis's call.
...Monch?
Within that plain room, everything stands in order – except the girl who always spoke with a hint of gloominess has disappeared.
He immediately spots a dagger sitting on the table in the middle of the room.
He gave that dagger to Monch.
He remains silent for a spell, then picks up the dagger and walks out the door.
The sun is shining outside.
Hey Doctor, look! They've already started selling Saintess-themed goods here.
My, if it isn't Lady Ensia. Welcome back to my humble shop.
You sure know how to keep up with the times.
When I came back to Kjerag, you were still selling Vine-Bead Court merch. Now you've switched to selling stuff with the Saintess on it.
Hahaha, I'm not the only one. Plenty of folks all over the streets. We're even being encouraged to do business now.
Now that you mention it, those things I was hawking before, I didn't actually believe in them.
But when I make goods for the Saintess, I have real faith in what I'm doing.
When we first heard about the transfer of power, us businessfolks were real worried about what was going to happen.
But I never imagined that the Saintess would be so open-minded.
Now, even the most stubborn of folk have nothing to say against dealing with foreigners.
Also, I heard that Sir Enciodes plans to meet with the Saintess for discussions to fully open Browntail and Paleroche territory to the outside world.
If you ask me, I'm looking forward to seeing this new day more and more.
Hehe.
I'm glad to hear you say that.
You certainly ought to be!
Take a look around you. Of all these people walking the street, who isn't smiling these days?
Even though all that craziness happened during the ceremony, the Saintess was able to mediate the situation among the three clans.
And that resulted in something most people could agree with.
Y'know, I have to thank the Saintess and Sir Enciodes for all this.
Oh right, were you planning on leaving Kjerag in that getup of yours?
Yup, my vacation's coming to an end, so it's time for me to head back to continue my treatment and work.
In that case, take whatever you want! It's on me!
Treat it like my parting gift to you!
Well...
No need to be polite. You've earned it.
Alright, I'll take you up on it then.
I want this portrait of my sister, this embroidery, and that sculpture.
Hey, that picture––
A painting hanging on the wall catches Ensia's attention.
It's of her sister Enya and her brother Enciodes, standing at a negotiating table and shaking hands, seemingly reaching some sort of agreement.
For a moment, she pauses.
Ah, this painting depicts Sir Enciodes and the Saintess reaching an agreement on Kjerag's future.
Though I don't know if this scene actually happened, there's no doubt that those two are the ones governing Kjerag now...
And everyone knows they're siblings.
Surely, they must get along very well.
Tell me, do they really?
Yup... Let's see if there's anything else...
Doctor, is there anything you want?

opts: I'd like some souvenirs too.

lines.x: You got it!
Um... Lady Ensia, I did say you could have anything you wanted for free, but please try to have some mercy...
...Doctor.
Though the two of us serve as Sir Enciodes's retainers, we are also employees of Rhodes Island.
We would like to apologize to you... for all sorts of things.
Courier and I will not shamelessly ask for your forgiveness. We only hope that we will be allowed to come to Rhodes Island to visit Lady Ensia in the future.
What do you think, Doctor?

opts: You were in a difficult situation. I understand.
I was a guest of the Paleroches. What does that have to do with you?

lines.x: This situation is indeed excusable, which I'll note in the report.
As for how the bosses will respond, that I cannot say.
Doctor, I know you don't assign any blame to them.
But playing dumb isn't very smart.
Coincidentally, you also resolved some of Enciodes's affairs.
If that's how you want to spin the account of what happened, then the report to Dr. Kal'tsit will take a bit of extra effort.
And I certainly won't help you with that.
Thank you... Doctor, and Captain Sharp.
Thanks again.
Doctor, I've already said goodbye to my folks. I'm ready to go now.
Got all your stuff?
Instructor Dobermann!
The Saintess became the de-facto leader of Kjerag, and Enciodes gained the power to leverage the majority of Kjerag's resources...
Doctor, you ought to take my advice.
I didn't hear your name mentioned by anyone else, fortunately. By the looks of things, you people managed to not get involved this time, at least.
Uhh...
By the looks of things, not only did you get yourselves involved, you got heavily involved.
I look forward to reading your reports.
Alright, if there's anything you have to say, save it for once we're on the road. Get in the car, we're heading back to Rhodes Island.
Doctor, do you remember that painting we saw in that store back there?

opts: The one with the handshake?
Not really.

lines.x: Mmhmm.
Doctor, stop playing dumb. I know you remember it.
Right then and there, I really wanted to buy that painting.
But after thinking about it some more, I decided not to. I mean, I know that never happened.
My brother just decided to let my sister have a move, that's all there was to it.

opts: Sorry.

lines.x: It's not your fault, Doctor.
Without you, the tension between those two would've only gotten more vicious.
But at least this helped me sort one thing out.
And that is – regardless of which one of them made the decision, it came as a result of their beliefs.
You were just a catalyst in driving things forward.
Just like how nobody would try to convince me to give up mountain climbing.
Beliefs can't be so easily changed.

opts: They wouldn't be called beliefs if they could.

lines.x: Right.
But, I have another belief that won't be easily changed.
One day, I'm going to make sure my brother and sister get along again, like they used to.

opts: (Enya and Enciodes.)
(Perhaps the perils that Kjerag faces have only just begun.)
